meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: American tourists arrested in Japan after alleged break-in at viral monkey Punch’s enclosure
items.summary: Two Americans were reportedly arrested at Ichikawa City Zoo in Japan after one allegedly trespassed into viral monkey star Punch's enclosure this week.

items.title: Maduro ally deported to US over alleged billion-dollar corruption scheme tied to oil, food program
items.summary: Maduro ally Alex Saab faces federal charges and deportation to the U.S. for allegedly orchestrating a sweeping money laundering and bribery scheme.

items.title: Bodies of four missing Italian divers found inside 'shark cave' in Maldives days after they vanished
items.summary: Rescuers found four Italian divers' bodies deep inside a Maldives underwater cave days after they vanished during a dive far beyond recreational limits.

items.title: US, Nigeria strike ISIS fighters again from the air after killing senior leader
items.summary: U.S. and Nigerian forces struck ISIS targets in Nigeria days after President Trump announced the killing of global ISIS leader Abu-Bilal al-Minuki.

items.title: Canada confirms hantavirus case linked to cruise ship outbreak that has killed three passengers
items.summary: Canada confirms a hantavirus case linked to the MV Hondius cruise ship outbreak that has killed three people and sickened multiple passengers.

items.title: Netanyahu 'blunder' threatens US-backed Israel-UAE alliance at critical moment with Iran: analyst
items.summary: U.S.-brokered Israel-UAE alliance shows signs of strain after Netanyahu's claimed secret UAE visit drew a swift denial amid rising Iran tensions.

items.title: ISIS terror leader at large after US strike kills top commander amid rising Africa threat: analyst
items.summary: Human intelligence penetrated deep local networks to kill ISIS West Africa commander al-Minuki, described as the group's biggest loss in years.

items.title: NATO ally Poland warns Russia, Belarus pushing illegal migrants toward alliance — and the US
items.summary: Poland's military guards its Belarus border against what officials call a Russian-backed hybrid warfare campaign using migrants to destabilize NATO.

items.title: Maldives military diver dies searching for four Italian divers missing inside underwater cave system
items.summary: A Maldivian military diver died from decompression sickness during a perilous mission to recover bodies of four Italian divers trapped in a deep underwater cave.

items.title: King Charles 'shocked and saddened' over soldier's 'unexplained' death at horse show he attended
items.summary: King Charles was "shocked and saddened" after a soldier died following a fall at the Royal Windsor Horse Show during a King's Troop display Friday.

items.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in Central Africa a public health emergency after 80 suspected deaths
items.summary: The WHO declared the Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda an international public health emergency after dozens of suspected deaths were reported.

items.title: Man dies after attack by 13-foot great white shark near popular island resort: officials
items.summary: A 38-year-old man was killed in a shark attack near Rottnest Island off Australia's western coast, authorities say, urging additional caution in the area.

items.title: UK defense shortfalls highlighted as Britain avoids Iran offensive role amid Trump criticism
items.summary: While Starmer opposed joining offensive action against Iran, reports suggest politics aside, Britain’s military may lack the capacity for a major combat role.

items.title: Driver identified after car plows into crowd injuring 8; passerby stabbed
items.summary: Salim El Koudri, 31, allegedly drove a car into a crowd of people in the Italian city of Modena on Saturday and then got out and slashed a citizen with a knife.

items.title: Trump says Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, second in command of ISIS globally, killed in US-Nigerian operation
items.summary: President Trump announced U.S. and Nigerian forces eliminated Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, whom he identified as the second in command of ISIS globally.

items.title: New Ebola outbreak leaves 65 dead as officials warn of cross-border spread
items.summary: A new Ebola outbreak in Congo has killed 65 people with 246 suspected cases as officials investigate which strain is driving the deadly spread.

items.title: Lebanon accuses Iran of inserting IRGC terrorists into country 'under guise of diplomatic activity'
items.summary: Lebanon reportedly filed a precedent-setting UN complaint against Iran amid U.S.-facilitated Israel-Lebanon normalization talks in Washington, D.C.

items.title: Mother recounts horrors of brutal Chinese detention camp where infant son died
items.summary: Uyghur mother Mihrigul Tursun recounts years of alleged detention, torture and family separation in China's Xinjiang camps in emotional testimony.

items.title: Ship seized off coast of UAE near Strait of Hormuz may have been 'floating armory': report
items.summary: UKMTO reports a ship was boarded by unauthorized personnel near the Strait of Hormuz off the UAE coast and headed toward Iranian territorial waters.

items.title: Non-Jewish professor says he was fired for calling out Hamas supporters in online post
items.summary: Canadian professor Paul Finlayson says he was fired for defending Israel on social media amid a wave of antisemitism following the Oct. 7 attacks.

items.title: Hamas used sexual violence 'deliberately and systematically' on Oct 7, commission report finds
items.summary: Investigators say Hamas perpetrators deliberately used sexual and gender-based violence as a weapon during the Oct. 7 massacres in southern Israel.

items.title: Trump pledges to raise detained pastor's case with Xi Jinping during Beijing visit as family pleads for help
items.summary: Pastor Ezra Jin's daughter speaks about her father's detention in China and Trump's pledge to raise his case with Xi Jinping during Beijing visit.

items.title: What Xi wants from Trump as Beijing seeks leverage in high-stakes summit
items.summary: President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing for a high-stakes summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping at a moment when both Washington and Beijing are trying to stabilize one of the world’s most consequential rivalries.

items.title: Iran says its small subs deployed to Strait of Hormuz as expert explains threat: ‘Vulnerable to detection’
items.summary: Iran claims it has deployed small submarines as an "invisible guardian" of the Strait of Hormuz, but analyst says the vessels pose limited strategic threat.

items.title: Trump faces split among retired US commanders over whether to resume Iran strikes
items.summary: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on "massive life support" as retired U.S. commanders debate whether to resume military operations against Tehran.
